name: advance-delivery
title: Advance Delivery
purpose: Capability: Execution-layer contract for ADV — TDD execution routing, task-run ledger, bulk operations, delta operations, and per-task checkpoint commits. Split from `advance` capability; absorbs former `contract-system` and `checkpoint-commits` capabilities.

requirements.title: Canonical Apply Tool Path for Inline TDD
requirements.body: For ordinary inline TDD work, /adv-apply MUST explicitly name editing tools (edit, write, morph_edit where appropriate) as the correct path for creating or modifying test files, and MUST explicitly name adv_run_test as the primary red/green test execution path.

scenarios.title: Red and green contract lines name adv_run_test
given: The /adv-apply command contract is present
scenarios.when: Its Red and Green phase guidance is inspected
then: The Red phase line names adv_run_test with phase 'red'
The Green phase line names adv_run_test with phase 'green'

scenarios.title: Apply contract names editing tools for test-file changes
given: The /adv-apply command contract is present
scenarios.when: Its ordinary inline-TDD file-editing guidance is inspected
then: At least one supported editing tool is named for test-file creation or modification
Raw shell file-writing is not presented as the normal path

requirements.title: Apply Contract Regression Anchors
requirements.body: The repository MUST maintain asset or regression tests that fail when canonical inline-TDD path wording is removed from /adv-apply or when shell-authored test-file content is reintroduced as normal-path guidance.

scenarios.title: Asset tests guard canonical path wording
given: The /adv-apply contract is covered by asset tests
scenarios.when: Canonical Red/Green adv_run_test wording is removed
then: The asset or regression suite fails

scenarios.title: Asset tests guard anti-pattern row
given: The /adv-apply contract is covered by asset tests
scenarios.when: The shell-authored test-file anti-pattern row is removed or weakened
then: The asset or regression suite fails

requirements.title: Runtime Enforcement for Inline-TDD Bash Workarounds
requirements.body: During active inline-TDD work, runtime enforcement MUST treat shell-authored test-file content and direct test-runner bash differently. Shell-authored test-file content targeting test-glob paths is prohibited and MUST be blocked. Direct test-runner bash without a matching recent adv_run_test signal MUST emit an advisory but MUST NOT be blocked. These are firm defaults, not feature-flagged rollout modes.

scenarios.title: Shell-authored test-file content is blocked
given: A change is active
An inline-TDD task is in progress
A bash command writes file content to a test-glob path
scenarios.when: The bash command is checked by runtime enforcement
then: The command is blocked
The error tells the agent to use editing tools and adv_run_test instead

scenarios.title: Direct test-runner bash is advisory only
given: A change is active
An inline-TDD task is in progress
A bash command runs a test runner without a matching recent adv_run_test signal
scenarios.when: The bash command is checked by runtime enforcement
then: The command is not blocked
An advisory is emitted directing the agent to prefer adv_run_test

requirements.title: Evidence Tool Value Justification
requirements.body: ADV command and instruction guidance MUST justify prescribed evidence tooling by durable user value. adv_run_test is the normal inline TDD path because it provides executable proof, durable evidence, and durable workflow-queryable test records. Retired fallback evidence tools MUST NOT be reintroduced as ordinary inline-TDD ceremony.

scenarios.title: adv_run_test prescription names value categories
given: /adv-apply or agent guidance prescribes adv_run_test for inline TDD
then: It explains executable proof value
It explains durable evidence value
It explains durable workflow-queryable test record value

requirements.title: Prescriptive Tool Guidance Must Pass Value-vs-Burden Test
requirements.body: New prescriptive ADV execution guidance MUST state the durable value category that justifies its burden. Valid categories include reproducibility, durable audit, recovery, safety, validation, and governance. Guidance that only adds ceremony without one of these values MUST NOT be added.

scenarios.title: Prescriptive guidance includes value category
given: A command or agent instruction tells the agent it MUST use a specific tool or workflow step
then: The guidance states at least one durable value category
The category explains why the burden is useful to the user or future recovery

scenarios.title: Ceremony without durable value is rejected
given: Proposed guidance adds an extra tool call or diagnostic surface
scenarios.when: It does not improve reproducibility, durable audit, recovery, safety, validation, or governance
then: The guidance is not added
Existing guidance is kept focused on value-producing evidence

requirements.title: Filter-Aware Bulk Close
requirements.body: adv_change_bulk_close must support closing multiple changes in a single approved tool call, using either an explicit ID list or filter-based selection, with fail-all semantics on invalid targets and a structured result envelope.

scenarios.title: Explicit ID list close
given: A list of valid active change IDs
scenarios.when: adv_change_bulk_close is called with kind: 'explicit' and those IDs
then: All specified changes are closed with the provided reason
The result envelope lists each change with success or error status
If reason: 'superseded', at most one survivor change ID may be provided

scenarios.title: Filter-based close requires explicit filter
given: A repository with changes in various states
then: The call must supply either a status filter (draft or pending) OR a staleness filter (createdBefore or lastActivityBefore)
No implicit default status is applied

scenarios.title: Fail-all on protected targets
given: A bulk close request targeting a mix of valid and invalid changes
scenarios.when: Any resolved target is active, archived, closed, nonexistent, ambiguous, or duplicated
then: The entire request fails before any mutation
The error identifies every invalid target and its specific failure reason

scenarios.title: Empty match is a structured error
given: A filter-based bulk close that matches zero changes
then: A structured error is returned with success: false
The message clearly states that no changes matched the filter
No silent no-op occurs

scenarios.title: Result envelope mirrors task-cancel pattern
given: A bulk close request that partially or fully succeeds
then: The envelope contains success, closed (count), results (array of per-change entries), and message
Each per-change entry includes changeId, success, and optional error

scenarios.title: No hard delete
given: Any bulk close call
then: Changes are closed (status moved to closed) but never purged or hard-deleted
Audit metadata is preserved for every closed change

scenarios.title: Existing close signature unchanged
given: The existing adv_change_close tool
then: Its signature and behavior remain identical to before adv_change_bulk_close was added

requirements.title: Typed Delta Operations
requirements.body: The delta system MUST enforce type safety on modifications and support rename operations on requirements. Modifications must reject unknown keys; renames must update title (and optionally id) while preserving all other fields.

scenarios.title: Reject unknown keys on modify
given: A DeltaModifySchema object with unknown keys
scenarios.when: The schema is parsed by Zod
then: The parse operation MUST fail with a validation error

scenarios.title: Successful rename preserves other fields
given: An existing requirement
A rename delta targeting its ID
then: The requirement's title is updated
All other fields are preserved

requirements.title: Verified Checkpoint Ordering
requirements.body: Checkpoint commits MUST only represent verified task completion state. Verification runs before checkpoint, not after.

scenarios.title: Complete-mode checkpoint requires verification
given: A task is in the green phase with file changes
The agent calls `adv_task_checkpoint` with `mode: "complete"`
scenarios.when: The checkpoint tool evaluates the request
then: If `verification` is absent or empty, the checkpoint MUST fail with `errorClass: SEMANTIC`
If `verification` is present, the checkpoint proceeds to guard validation

scenarios.title: Red-phase checkpoint prohibited
given: A task has failing tests or unverified changes
scenarios.when: The agent attempts checkpoint before green phase
then: The checkpoint MUST NOT run
The agent retries or escalates per doom-loop protocol

requirements.title: Scope Guard
requirements.body: Checkpoint commits MUST be scoped to the correct change worktree. Branch and HEAD guards are fail-closed and run before staging.

scenarios.title: Branch mismatch blocks checkpoint
given: `expectedBranch` is provided and does not match `git branch --show-current`
then: The checkpoint MUST fail with structured mismatch details (`expectedBranch`, `actualBranch`)
Remediation guidance MUST include the correct branch name and checkout command
No files are staged or committed

scenarios.title: HEAD mismatch blocks checkpoint
given: `expectedHeadSha` is provided and does not match `git rev-parse HEAD`
then: The checkpoint MUST fail with structured mismatch details (`expectedHeadSha`, `actualHeadSha`)
Remediation guidance MUST include the correct HEAD SHA
No files are staged or committed

scenarios.title: Change identity derived from task
given: A valid `taskId` is provided
scenarios.when: The checkpoint tool resolves the change context
then: The tool MUST call `store.tasks.show(taskId)` to derive the owning `changeId`
If an optional caller `changeId` is provided, it MUST match the derived value
Mismatch MUST fail with `errorClass: SEMANTIC`

requirements.title: Audit Metadata
requirements.body: Checkpoint commits MUST include machine-readable metadata in the commit message for audit and rollback purposes.

scenarios.title: Structured commit message
given: A checkpoint succeeds
then: The commit message MUST have a terse subject line (`task({taskId}): {mode}`)
The commit body MUST include `Change: {changeId}`
The commit body MUST include `Task: {taskId}`
The commit body MUST include `Mode: {mode}`
When `verification` is present, the body MUST include `Verification: {verification}`
The message MUST be created with multiple `-m` args for subject + body/trailers

scenarios.title: Clean tree returns without commit
given: The working tree has no changes
then: No commit is created
The result MUST include `status: "clean"`
Branch/HEAD guards still validate when guard params are active

requirements.title: Dirty-Baseline Protection
requirements.body: The `/adv-apply` flow MUST capture a clean baseline before the Red Phase to ensure checkpoint commits represent only the task's incremental changes.

scenarios.title: Baseline capture before Red Phase
given: A task is starting in `/adv-apply`
scenarios.when: The agent begins the task workflow
then: The agent MUST verify the working tree is clean or checkpoint any pre-existing changes before entering Red Phase
The Red Phase MUST begin from a known-clean or checkpointed state

scenarios.title: Incremental verification before checkpoint
given: A task has completed the Green Phase
then: The agent MUST run incremental verification (tests, typecheck, lint) before calling `adv_task_checkpoint`
Verification output is passed as the `verification` argument

requirements.title: No-Publication Authority
requirements.body: Checkpoint commits are local rollback/audit points only. Publication, merge, and archive remain separate workflows requiring explicit user approval.

scenarios.title: Checkpoint commits are not published
given: A checkpoint commit exists in a change worktree
then: The agent MUST NOT push the checkpoint commit to a remote
The agent MUST NOT merge the checkpoint commit to the default branch
The agent MUST NOT treat the checkpoint as an archived release

scenarios.title: Archive is the separate publication path
given: All tasks are done and the acceptance gate is complete
then: `/adv-archive` runs Phase 9 Git Finalization as the publication path
Worktree cleanup is blocked until the branch is merged or the archive process completes
The user MUST explicitly approve archive sign-off

requirements.title: Checkpoint ledger recording blocks task completion when ledger write fails
requirements.body: adv_task_checkpoint MUST surface ledger recording failures via `checkpointRecorded: false` when the git commit (or clean-tree result) succeeds but the task-run ledger event cannot be persisted. /adv-apply MUST treat `checkpointRecorded: false` as blocking task completion: the agent runs adv_task_show, retries the checkpoint or ledger event, and only marks the task done after `checkpointRecorded: true` is observed. Returning `checkpointRecorded: false` MUST include actionable remediation guidance and MUST NOT be silently treated as success.

scenarios.title: Committed checkpoint with ledger failure surfaces checkpointRecorded:false
given: An /adv-apply task has dirty tree changes that pass branch and HEAD guards
The git commit phase succeeds but the task-run ledger event cannot be persisted
scenarios.when: adv_task_checkpoint executes in mode complete or cancel
then: The tool returns status `committed` with the new commit sha
The tool returns `checkpointRecorded: false` to indicate the ledger event was not durably recorded
The remediation guidance names adv_task_show and a retry path for ledger recovery
The result MUST NOT be silently treated as task completion

scenarios.title: Clean-tree checkpoint with ledger failure surfaces checkpointRecorded:false
given: An /adv-apply task has no dirty tree changes
The clean-tree path executes but the task-run ledger event cannot be persisted
then: The tool returns status `clean` with no new commit
The tool returns `checkpointRecorded: false` to indicate the ledger event was not durably recorded
The remediation guidance names adv_task_show and a retry path for ledger recovery

scenarios.title: Apply guidance treats checkpointRecorded:false as blocking task completion
given: An /adv-apply task has received `checkpointRecorded: false` from adv_task_checkpoint
scenarios.when: The agent prepares to mark the task done
then: The agent MUST run adv_task_show to inspect ledger state
The agent MUST retry the checkpoint or ledger event before marking the task done
The agent MUST NOT call adv_task_update with status done until checkpointRecorded:true is observed
Existing doom-loop and blocker semantics apply if recovery cannot be achieved within the retry budget

requirements.title: Archive Completeness Validation
requirements.body: adv_change_archive MUST run validateChange before creating the archive bundle. Validation errors block the archive; warnings are included in the response but do not block. Validation runs before the idempotent bundle-existence check so that retries also validate.

scenarios.title: Validation errors block archive
given: A change passing archive preflight (all tasks done/cancelled, all gates satisfied)
validateChange returns one or more errors
then: The archive is rejected with a structured error response
The error response includes validator error codes and messages
No archive bundle is written

scenarios.title: Validation warnings do not block archive
given: A change passing archive preflight
validateChange returns warnings but no errors
then: Validation warnings are included in the archive response
The archive proceeds normally

scenarios.title: Idempotent retry still validates
given: A previous archive attempt wrote a bundle but failed status transition
The bundle exists on disk
then: validateChange runs before the bundle-existence short-circuit
A previously-written bundle does not bypass validation

requirements.title: adv_change_show accepts opt-in include flags
requirements.body: adv_change_show must accept an optional include argument with shape { ledger?: boolean, snapshot?: boolean, readyTasks?: boolean, readyTasksLimit?: number }. When include is omitted, the response shape is unchanged (backward compatible). When include.snapshot is true, the rendered context snapshot is attached as top-level _contextSnapshot, matching the mutation-tool convention. When include.ledger is true, the in-progress task's TaskRunState (or null) is attached as _ledger. When include.readyTasks is true, the unblocked ready queue is attached as _readyTasks (top-N by priority then created_at; default 10, max 50) along with _readyTasksMeta. Cross-project target_path routing is preserved.

scenarios.title: Default behavior preserved when include is omitted
given: A change exists with completed and pending tasks
then: The response does NOT contain _contextSnapshot, _ledger, or _readyTasks
All previously documented fields remain unchanged

scenarios.title: include.snapshot attaches _contextSnapshot
given: A change exists
scenarios.when: adv_change_show is called with include: { snapshot: true }
then: The response includes top-level _contextSnapshot string
The snapshot uses the same formatter as live system-block emission

scenarios.title: include.readyTasksLimit accepts 1-50
given: A request includes readyTasks:true with readyTasksLimit:N
then: Values 1-50 inclusive are accepted
Values <1 or >50 are rejected at the schema layer

requirements.title: adv_status accepts optional view selector
requirements.body: adv_status must accept an optional view argument of type 'summary' | 'health' | 'changes' | 'hygiene' (default: 'summary'). The summary view returns specs.count, simplified changes.recent (id+title+recency+minutesSinceActivity only), recommendations, temporal_health_ok (boolean), and worktree_count — explicitly omitting hygiene-archaeology fields like _healthSnapshot. The health view returns full temporal_health, search_attributes, opencode_session_debt, diagnostics, and the metrics counters. The changes view returns full status.changes detail. The hygiene view returns _healthSnapshot, opencode_session_debt detail, project_metadata, recommendations, and migration_status. The formatted block is preserved across all views.

scenarios.title: Default view is summary
given: adv_status is called with no view arg
then: The response includes view:'summary'
Hygiene archaeology fields (_healthSnapshot, search_attributes, opencode_session_debt, diagnostics) are absent

scenarios.title: Health view exposes full diagnostics
given: adv_status is called with view:'health'
then: The response includes temporal_health, search_attributes, opencode_session_debt, diagnostics
Summary-only fields (temporal_health_ok, worktree_count) are absent

requirements.title: Per-phase metrics counters surfaced via health view
requirements.body: The plugin must maintain in-memory counters for the session: adv_tool_calls (total adv_* tool invocations), adv_tool_call_count_by_name (Record<string, number> breakdown), system_block_bytes (cumulative bytes appended to output.system[0]), subagent_spawns (count of `task` tool invocations from main), and wall_time_ms. Counters reset on plugin init. They are surfaced as the metrics field on adv_status view:'health'. Per JC-1, persistence across sessions is OUT of scope for this baseline.

scenarios.title: Counters reset on plugin init
given: The plugin factory runs
then: All five counters are zero
adv_tool_call_count_by_name is empty

scenarios.title: Health view exposes counters
given: adv_status is called with view:'health'
then: The response includes a metrics object with the five fields
The counters reflect activity since the most recent plugin init
